response.justification: Al-Said brings exceptional geospatial domain expertise through founding GISCollective and mapping projects—a rare asset for routing tiling work. However, his C++ experience is substantially dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments), while recent roles focus heavily on TypeScript/Node.js. His strong software craftsmanship culture, problem-solving approach, and demonstrated willingness to learn across technologies suggest potential for re-engaging with modern C++, but this represents a notable transition from his current career trajectory.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise (GISCollective founder, FOSS4G speaker, multiple mapping projects)
Strong software craftsmanship culture with emphasis on TDD, testing frameworks, and quality assurance
Proven ability to expand expertise across multiple languages and technical domains
concerns: C++ work is dated (2014-2015 at National Instruments, earlier thesis work from 2009)
Last 9 years of career focused on TypeScript/Node.js rather than systems programming languages
No explicit experience building routing guidance systems or navigation products

key_factors.factor: Recent tech stack focus on TypeScript vs systems languages
